query: Recent PLA aircraft activity around Taiwan ADIZ in May-June 2026, and reported pause or drop in activity. What are typical daily counts recently and highest single-day count in 2026?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings for the forecasting question on July–August 2026 max daily PLA aircraft in Taiwan's ADIZ:

## Key Takeaways

**The "pause" premise is well-supported.** 2026 has featured genuine multi-day zero-activity stretches:
- **Feb 27–Mar 5, 2026:** ~7 days of ZERO PLA aircraft (the marquee pause event)
- **June 27–28, 2026:** near-zero (1 aircraft, then 0)

**2026 activity is dramatically lower than 2024–2025.** The Taiwan Security Monitor cites a **2026 daily average of ~4.5 aircraft** — a steep drop from the 2024–2025 tempo (>4,000 incursions Jan–Sep 2025 alone).

**Typical recent daily counts** cluster in the **single digits to low teens**, with periodic surges into the **20s–30s** (tied to combat-readiness patrols/exercises).

**Highest single-day count in 2026:** The confirmed maxima in news reporting are **32 aircraft** (Feb 12 and June 4, 2026). Analyst framing (March 14's 26 being "only the 8th largest of 2026") suggests the year's top days sit in the **low-to-mid 30s** — far below 2024–2025 peaks (2024 single-day record ~153; Aug 2025 had 80+ days). I could not directly access the CSIS ChinaPower day-level table to confirm the absolute annual maximum, so it could be marginally higher.

**Immediate trajectory into July:** Late June was very quiet (0–1 aircraft), but early July rebounded — Jul 1 = 13, Jul 3 = 30 — showing the characteristic episodic surge pattern rather than a sustained decline.

Full structured data (all daily counts, sources, and data points) has been submitted via the StructuredOutput tool.

sources.title: All Quiet in the Taiwan Strait? Explaining the Recent Drop in PLA Aircraft Activity Around Taiwan
sources.snippet: From February 27th to March 5th, Taiwan's MND reported no PLA aircraft operating near the ADIZ — an unusually long pause. Before the pause: Jan 29 = 19, Feb 12 = 32, Feb 26 = 22 aircraft.

sources.title: From 16 Aircraft to Surrounded: Fear, Virality, and the Misinformation Cascade in ADIZ Discourse
sources.snippet: March 14 had 26 aircraft (16 entering ADIZ), ~5.78x 2026's daily average of 4.5; it was only the 8th largest ADIZ incursion of 2026. Low activity resumed March 15.

sources.title: Taiwan reports heightened Chinese military activity around territory (June 4, 2026)
sources.snippet: June 4: 32 sorties of PLA aircraft, 10 PLAN vessels and 5 official ships; 25 of 32 crossed the median line.

sources.title: Taiwan tracks 22 military aircraft, 10 Chinese ships (June 6, 2026)
sources.snippet: June 6: MND tracked 22 Chinese military aircraft, 8 naval vessels, 2 official ships.

sources.title: Taiwan detects 1 sortie of Chinese military aircraft, 7 vessels (June 27, 2026)
sources.snippet: June 27: 1 sortie of PLA aircraft and 7 PLAN vessels detected.

sources.title: Taiwan Detects Hike in Chinese Military Incursions (June 28, 2026)
sources.snippet: June 28: 6 PLAN vessels; no PLA aircraft detected during the timeframe.

sources.title: Taiwan detects uptick in Chinese incursions around itself (June 25, 2026)
sources.snippet: June 25: 14 sorties of PLA aircraft, 6 naval vessels, 2 official ships; 10 crossed median line.

sources.title: Taiwan detects rise in Chinese military incursions (July 1, 2026)
sources.snippet: July 1: 13 sorties of PLA aircraft, 10 PLAN vessels, 3 official ships; 9 crossed median line.

sources.title: Taiwan detects 5 Chinese aircraft, 6 naval vessels; 2 cross median line (July 2, 2026)
sources.snippet: July 2: 5 sorties of PLA aircraft, 6 naval vessels, 5 official ships; 2 crossed median line.

sources.title: Taiwan Detects Surge in Chinese Military Activity (July 3, 2026)
sources.snippet: July 3: 30 sorties of PLA aircraft, 7 naval vessels, 5 official ships; 26 crossed median line.

sources.title: China's Y-20 Tanker Intercepted while Refueling J-16 Fighters Near Taiwan
sources.snippet: Between May 24 and 26, China flew nearly 60 aircraft/helicopters/drones in Taiwan's ADIZ; 21 aircraft tracked May 25 including a Y-20 tanker refueling J-16s.

sources.title: Taiwan detects 10 sorties of Chinese military aircraft, 7 vessels (May 27, 2026)
sources.snippet: May 27: 10 sorties of PLA aircraft, 7 naval vessels; 9 crossed median line.

sources.title: Taiwan detects 6 sorties of Chinese military aircraft, 10 vessels (May 22, 2026)
sources.snippet: May 22: 6 sorties of PLA aircraft, 10 vessels; all 6 crossed median line.

sources.title: Taiwan ADIZ Violations | ChinaPower Project - CSIS
sources.snippet: Authoritative dataset by Gerald C. Brown and Ben Lewis tracking Chinese air incursions into Taiwan's ADIZ (day-level table not directly accessible via fetch).

sources.title: Taiwan Incursion Updates
sources.snippet: Total air incursions into ADIZ: 2022 = 1,737; 2023 = 1,714; 2024 = 444 (median-line crossings metric varies by source).

data_points.metric: 2026 daily average PLA aircraft in/near Taiwan ADIZ
PLA aircraft tracked May 25, 2026 (Y-20 tanker event)
Consecutive days with zero PLA aircraft (Feb 27–Mar 5, 2026 pause)
